per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA requires assessing impact early by completing an Impact Assessment, while the REG fragment only states that 'shortcomings can be addressed'; these do not clearly express the same or directly conflicting constraint.

The REA describes performing an Impact Assessment, while the REG fragment merely introduces that 'Those practices shall concern in particular:' and does not state a comparable substantive requirement.

The REA mandates an early Impact Assessment, whereas the REG says testing procedures 'may include testing in real-world conditions'; these concern different actions and there is no quote-supported conflict.
